app.name: Glean
app.bestFor: Enterprise workplace search and RAG across many connected tools; strongest for broad cross-app knowledge retrieval.

app.name: Guru
app.bestFor: AI knowledge base that surfaces verified answers inside your workflow with source trust/verification built in.

app.name: Notion AI
app.bestFor: Teams already in Notion who want RAG Q&A over their own docs and wiki without standing up separate infra.

app.name: Confluence + Atlassian Intelligence
app.bestFor: Atlassian shops that want AI search and answers layered onto existing Confluence documentation.

app.name: Dashworks
app.bestFor: AI assistant that answers questions across all connected company apps without pre-indexing everything.

app.name: CustomGPT.ai
app.bestFor: No-code RAG deployment with anti-hallucination controls; pitched at compliance-heavy and regulated orgs.

skill.name: LangChain
skill.bestFor: The de-facto framework for building RAG and knowledge-base apps — chains, agents, and connectors to every model and vector store. The category's recognized leader (~142k stars).

skill.name: Dify
skill.bestFor: Open-source LLM app platform with a built-in knowledge base, document ingestion, and RAG pipeline behind a full UI; the highest-starred option here (~150k stars).

skill.name: RAGFlow
skill.bestFor: Dedicated open-source RAG engine with deep document parsing, knowledge-base management, and a zero-code web UI (~86k stars).

skill.name: AnythingLLM
skill.bestFor: All-in-one desktop/self-hosted app that turns your documents into a private chat knowledge base out of the box (~64k stars).

skill.name: LlamaIndex
skill.bestFor: The data framework for indexing and querying your private content; the reference toolkit for document-QA knowledge bases (~51k stars).

skill.name: obsidian-skills (kepano)
skill.bestFor: Official Obsidian Agent Skills that teach an agent to read/write your Markdown vault, Bases, and Canvas as a knowledge base (~43k stars).

skill.name: Quivr
skill.bestFor: Opinionated RAG 'second brain' you can plug into your apps to store and query your documents (~39k stars).

skill.name: Khoj
skill.bestFor: Self-hostable AI second brain that searches and chats across your notes, docs, and PDFs (~36k stars).

skill.name: Haystack (deepset)
skill.bestFor: deepset's modular, production-grade pipelines for search and RAG over your knowledge base (~26k stars).

skill.name: claude-obsidian (AgriciDaniel)
skill.bestFor: Self-organizing second-brain skill set: drop any source and Claude reads, links, and files it into one connected Markdown knowledge graph (~9.9k stars).

skill.name: obsidian-second-brain (eugeniughelbur)
skill.bestFor: Cross-CLI skill turning a vault into an AI-first knowledge base with hybrid semantic search and self-maintaining notes (~3.5k stars).

notes: Category is contaminated by vendor blogs that rank their own product #1 (e.g. CustomGPT.ai, Chitika) — those are weighted down in the app track. The Skill/Repo track is now ranked by real quality, reputation, and adoption (GitHub stars) rather than form factor — kind ('skill' vs 'repo') is only a tag, so a hugely-adopted RAG repo outranks a niche skill. It mixes the notable open-source RAG/knowledge-base frameworks (LangChain, Dify, RAGFlow, AnythingLLM, LlamaIndex, Quivr, Khoj, Haystack) with genuine Agent Skills for a personal knowledge base (kepano's official Obsidian skills and the Obsidian second-brain skill family). Star counts verified via the GitHub API (Jul 2026).
